label: fiction
title: The Purple Label
setting: A meal-preparation contamination trial in 2026, where a personal grudge competes with a packaging printer that reused yesterday’s allergen label.
charge: Intentionally contaminating prepared food and causing serious injury
elements: The accused performed or participated in the alleged act.
The act was deliberate or dishonest rather than accidental.
The prosecution has proved every required fact beyond reasonable doubt.
hook: The purple label promises safety. Inside the sealed meal is the one ingredient the customer cannot survive.

accused.human: Rina Dovel runs free weekend cooking classes and supports a child with severe allergies at home.
accused.if_guilty: A conviction means prison, loss of food-safety work, and public branding as someone who weaponised the danger she understands personally.

prosecution.speaker: pc
prosecution.text: The prosecution says the accused threatened a former friend, selected the dangerous ingredient, and signed the batch that sent the meal out. The apparent centre of the case is an angry message sent by the accused, but the evidence must be followed through the complete sequence rather than treated as a single dramatic clue.

defence.speaker: dc
defence.text: The defence says the threat was private anger while a documented printer fault placed a safe label on the wrong meal during a rushed shift. Suspicion gathered around a person before investigators tested the limits of an angry message sent by the accused; those limits matter because a criminal verdict cannot rest on an attractive shortcut.

prosecution.speaker: pc
prosecution.text: The prosecution began with Rina’s angry message and shift signature, but it does not ask you to convict on that clue alone. Rina supervised the line, handled ingredient tubs near the packing area, and sent a threat shortly before Orin received the dangerous meal. The defence has exposed real limits in the first impression; those limits deserve weight, not automatic control of the verdict. Consider the records together, apply the judge’s direction to every element, and return guilty only if this connected evidence leaves you sure beyond reasonable doubt.

defence.speaker: dc
defence.text: The defence accepts that Rina’s angry message and shift signature created suspicion, but suspicion is not the test. The disconnected printer reused an old safe-label template, and three other meals packed in the same minute carried mismatched labels. That evidence goes to the heart of the case because anger and supervisory responsibility do not prove that Rina relabelled this tray deliberately. The accused need not identify another offender. If this competing account remains reasonably possible, the only lawful verdict is not guilty.

epilogue: The injured customer recovered slowly and accepted Rina’s apology for the message, not for a crime. The kitchen introduced scan-to-pack checks, and Rina returned only after completing additional safety training beside the worker who found the printer fault. The people involved kept living with the consequences after the courtroom emptied. The case changed workplace rules, but it could not return the months spent under accusation.

beats.speaker: pc
beats.text: The sealed meal contained ground nut paste while its purple label declared the batch free from that ingredient. Rina signed the shift sheet covering packing and dispatch. At this first point, the exhibit’s stated limit is that anger and supervisory responsibility do not prove that Rina relabelled this tray deliberately, so it must be weighed with the rest of the sequence.
beats.reveal_note: The first impression came from Rina’s angry message and shift signature; its value depended on whether the later evidence could overcome this limit: anger and supervisory responsibility do not prove that Rina relabelled this tray deliberately.

beats.speaker: w2
beats.text: Rina and I had ended a friendship badly. She messaged that I would regret humiliating her, then my throat closed after three bites of a meal carrying the safe label. This was the second fact I recorded in the initial The Purple Label inquiry, before investigators settled on Rina Dovel. The timing was noted before the The Purple Label inquiry settled on Rina Dovel as its suspect.
beats.reveal_note: This witness fixed the practical setting before suspicion centred on Rina Dovel, giving the room context for the records that followed.

beats.speaker: w1
beats.text: You initially called the wrong label deliberate? Yes, before the other three meals were found. Those meals showed both kinds of mismatch? They did, which was not consistent with targeting one customer. The three additional mismatches turned a targeted-looking label into evidence of a wider system fault.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: You initially called the wrong label deliberate?

turns.speaker: w1
turns.text: Yes, before the other three meals were found.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: Those meals showed both kinds of mismatch?

turns.speaker: w1
turns.text: They did, which was not consistent with targeting one customer. The three additional mismatches turned a targeted-looking label into evidence of a wider system fault.

beats.reveal_note: The challenged record mattered because it exposed the gap between appearance and proof: anger and supervisory responsibility do not prove that Rina relabelled this tray deliberately.

beats.speaker: pc
beats.text: The kitchen camera shows Rina placing ingredient tubs near the line. It does not show her opening this meal or changing a label after the tray was sealed. At this fourth point, the exhibit’s stated limit is that anger and supervisory responsibility do not prove that Rina relabelled this tray deliberately, so it must be weighed with the rest of the sequence.
beats.reveal_note: Cross-examination narrowed the early clue without erasing it, forcing the room to decide how much independent support remained.

beats.speaker: dc
beats.text: The printer lost its network connection at 11:42. When that model reconnects, it can repeat the last cached label unless the operator clears the local queue. At this fifth point, the exhibit’s stated limit is that anger and supervisory responsibility do not prove that Rina relabelled this tray deliberately, so it must be weighed with the rest of the sequence.
beats.reveal_note: The strongest prosecution point was this: Rina supervised the line, handled ingredient tubs near the packing area, and sent a threat shortly before Orin received the dangerous meal.

beats.speaker: w3
beats.text: Three surviving meals packed within the same minute had wrong labels: two ordinary meals marked safe and one safe meal marked as containing the ingredient. This was the sixth fact I recorded in the initial The Purple Label inquiry, before investigators settled on Rina Dovel. In the The Purple Label inquiry, I separated what I personally observed from what investigators later asked me to infer.
beats.reveal_note: The competing account was concrete rather than speculative: the disconnected printer reused an old safe-label template, and three other meals packed in the same minute carried mismatched labels.

beats.speaker: w4
beats.text: I signed the shift sheet because I supervised six lines. I sent an ugly message after an argument, but I did not touch Orin’s tray or try to hurt anyone. This was the seventh fact I recorded in the initial The Purple Label inquiry, before investigators settled on Rina Dovel. That The Purple Label detail can be checked against the contemporaneous record rather than accepted as a later reconstruction.
beats.reveal_note: This testimony tested whether the system record described a person’s conduct or only an event produced around them.

beats.speaker: w2
beats.text: You believed the message referred to this meal? I did because it arrived the night before. Rina never mentioned food or an allergy? No. She said I would regret humiliating her. The message was ugly and relevant to motive, but it never referred to food, labels, or an allergy.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: You believed the message referred to this meal?

turns.speaker: w2
turns.text: I did because it arrived the night before.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: Rina never mentioned food or an allergy?

turns.speaker: w2
turns.text: No. She said I would regret humiliating her. The message was ugly and relevant to motive, but it never referred to food, labels, or an allergy.

beats.reveal_note: The second cross put the decisive inference under pressure and showed exactly which part of the account could still be disputed.

beats.speaker: w3
beats.text: You knew the printer had dropped offline? I saw the warning but thought it had recovered. You did not clear the local queue? No. The manual did not tell operators to do that after reconnection. The operator saw a warning, yet the manual did not explain that yesterday’s queue could resume after reconnection.

turns.speaker: pc
turns.text: You knew the printer had dropped offline?

turns.speaker: w3
turns.text: I saw the warning but thought it had recovered.

turns.speaker: pc
turns.text: You did not clear the local queue?

turns.speaker: w3
turns.text: No. The manual did not tell operators to do that after reconnection. The operator saw a warning, yet the manual did not explain that yesterday’s queue could resume after reconnection.

beats.reveal_note: The final witness supplied a separate check on the timeline, reducing the risk that one attractive clue would decide the case alone.

beats.speaker: judge
beats.text: The prosecution must prove every element beyond reasonable doubt. The accused does not have to prove an alternative account, explain every suspicious detail, or show who else was responsible. Treat digital records like any other evidence: examine their origin, limits, and connection to a person.
beats.reveal_note: The direction kept the burden where it belonged: anger and supervisory responsibility do not prove that Rina relabelled this tray deliberately, and no gap in the defence could substitute for proof of every element.

twist: Rina’s message was cruel but did not describe food. The label printer had reissued yesterday’s safe template after losing connection, and three other meals from the same minute carried mismatched ingredient labels.
